title: Media Advisory | Four years after major summit, UNESCO convenes global leaders to tackle the education financing crisis
content:
PRESS INVITATION Four years after major summit, UNESCO convenes global leaders to tackle the education financing crisis Four years after the landmark Transforming Education Summit convened by the UN Secretary-General, UNESCO will bring together education leaders in Paris on 10 July to examine global progress and challenges in education. Participants will include President Cyril Ramaphosa [...]

title: Deaths in US immigration custody must be investigated: UN rights chief
content:
UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Friday called for stronger independent oversight of the United States immigration detention system and investigations into all deaths in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Deaths in US immigration custody must be investigated: UN rights chief appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Press Release | 655 million people still living without electricity underscore urgent need to deliver on universal energy access target
content:
JOINT PRESS RELEASE 655 million people still living without electricity underscore urgent need to deliver on universal energy access target Latest data show uneven progress, highlighting the critical need to focus on affordability and vulnerable communities   Washington, New York, Paris, Geneva, Abu Dhabi, 24 June 2026 – At a time when energy security and affordability have [...]

title: Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response
content:
The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to spread faster than aid efforts can keep pace, despite significant gains in treatment capacity and growing community engagement, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
content:
Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.  Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Press Release | UN Public Service Awards to honor 12 initiatives for accelerating progress on the Sustainable Development Goals
content:
